100 Years Ago Today, This is What Was Happening ...

On July 7, 1926, the International Chamber of Commerce met in Paris, discussing global trade recovery post-World War I. In the United States, the Senate passed a bill to improve rural mail service, reflecting a growing commitment to enhancing communication in remote areas. Meanwhile, reports surfaced of escalating tensions in the Middle East, as conflicts over borders and governance continued to simmer.
